summary: Verified the Bearer-token auth bug fix end-to-end. Backend /auth/login now returns access_token in body; protected endpoints accept Authorization: Bearer header. Frontend stores token in localStorage as 'taskflow_access_token', admin dashboard fully loads with all 4 KPIs, persists across reload, logout clears token + cookies, and protected routes redirect to /login. Employee login lands on /tasks with kanban columns rendered.
passed: POST /api/auth/login -> 200, body contains user fields + access_token (245-char JWT)
GET /api/auth/me with Authorization: Bearer -> 200 + user payload (no cookie used)
GET /api/users with Bearer -> 200, list of users
GET /api/tasks?scope=all with Bearer -> 200, list of tasks
Admin UI login lands on /admin and KPIs (kpi-employees=5, kpi-total-tasks=8, kpi-completed=4, kpi-hours-logged=11.0) render; no 'Loading dashboard…' stuck state
Reload persistence: dashboard re-renders after browser reload (token survives)
Logout clears localStorage token and redirects to /login; subsequent /admin nav redirects to /login
Employee login (maya@taskflow.com) lands on /tasks with To Do / In Progress / Done kanban columns

minor_observations.issue: Recharts console warning 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' for the Status breakdown pie (container initial size). Cosmetic/pre-existing, not related to auth fix. Chart legend renders but pie may render empty until container measured.
